model: ROG Gladius III Wireless AimPoint EVA-02
snap_subtitle: A true Evangelion collab that performs as hard as it looks.
game2: Zone of the Enders: The 2nd Runner
keytakeaway: The ROG Gladius III Wireless AimPoint EVA-02 Edition is a rare example of a collaboration that keeps *all* the flagship hardware intact. Built on ASUS’s AimPoint sensor platform and featuring the Push-Fit Switch Socket II, it pairs top-tier performance with Evangelion collector styling that feels authentic to the series. A true “usable merch” mouse—comfortable, repairable, and competitive enough for ranked play—scoring **0.1 higher** than the standard AimPoint for its cohesive EVA execution and included grip tape.
pcard: The ROG Gladius III Wireless AimPoint EVA-02 Edition merges flagship gaming hardware with Evangelion-grade style. You get the same 36,000 DPI AimPoint sensor, Tri-mode SpeedNova wireless, and **Push-Fit Switch Socket II** system found in ASUS’s top models—wrapped in Unit-02 reds and oranges with matching grip tape for traction and display value. It’s not just a paint job; it’s a continuation of ASUS’s Evangelion collaboration series (EVA-01 → EVA-02), and a rare collab that’s as functional as it is collectible. If you prefer a lighter pure-performance alternative, check the <pop=mouse-123>Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro</pop>, but for repairability and style, this one stands alone.
verdict:
The ROG Gladius III Wireless AimPoint EVA-02 is a wireless ergonomic gaming mouse built for players who want collectible design **and** dependable performance in shooters and MOBAs. Under the Asuka-coded reds/oranges sits ASUS’s AimPoint optical sensor (tuned PAW3395) with steady 1,000 Hz wireless behavior, tri-mode connectivity, and the brand’s unique **Push-Fit Switch Socket II** for solder-free main-switch swaps (mechanical *or* optical). Battery life is a highlight too, comfortably stretching past the 100-hour mark in 2.4 GHz mode with lighting off.

#### What you’ll feel
This is a tall, palm-filling right-hand shape (123 × 68 × 44 mm) that favors comfort and stability over “featherweight twitch.” At **79 g**, glide feels planted and predictable—great for [**Overwatch 2**](/games/overwatch-2) style tracking and angle-holding in [**Valorant**](/games/valorant). The higher click height and rear hump support medium-to-large hands in relaxed palm or a laid-back claw. Fingertip users or smaller hands may feel guided by the shell rather than fully in control.

| Aspect | What you’ll notice |
|---|---|
| Shape/weight | More anchored than hyper-nimble; aids smooth tracking and long sessions |
| Sensor feel | Consistent across common DPI (400–1600) with stable motion at 1K polling (capped at 1000 Hz) |
| Connectivity | SpeedNova 2.4 for games, Bluetooth for work, USB-C for play-and-charge |
| Endurance | Real-world “charge once a week+” usage with RGB off |

> Hot-swappable main switches make this one of the few “collector” mice you can realistically keep alive for years.

#### Highlights
- **AimPoint sensor + SpeedNova 2.4 GHz:** dependable, drama-free tracking at 1K polling.  
- **Tri-mode (2.4 / BT / USB-C):** bounce between a gaming rig and a laptop with zero hassle.  
- **Push-Fit Switch Socket II:** drop in mechanical **or** optical switches to customize feel or kill double-clicks.  
- **EVA-02 execution:** themed shell plus matching grip tape that actually boosts traction.  
- **Battery life:** rated ~119 hours on 2.4 GHz (lighting off) reduces charge anxiety.  

#### Why this collab matters (rarity & execution)
- **True collaboration, not a reskin:** you’re getting the *full* Gladius III AimPoint hardware with cohesive EVA-02 theming (colors, typography, in-box grip tape).  
- **Part of an ongoing ASUS x Evangelion partnership:** following the earlier **EVA-01 Edition**, the EVA-02 AimPoint continues the themed ROG lineup (GPUs, boards, and peripherals) that blend performance with anime heritage.  
- **Collector-first details:** included tape adds real traction and preserves the aesthetic; the theme extends beyond a shell swap.  
- **Usable merch:** unlike many collabs, this is spec-complete for ranked play—so it’s display-worthy *and* mainable.  

#### Tradeoffs to know
- **Mass & posture:** at 79 g with a tall hump, it won’t feel as effortless to lift/redirect as sub-60 g esport shells.  
- **Scroll wheel lottery:** the encoder isn’t part of the hotswap system and can develop “kickback” over time on some units.  
- **Armoury Crate heft:** great for firmware/profiles; most players will set onboard memory then close it.  
- **Polling headroom:** capped at 1000 Hz—fine for most, but not for 4K/8K chasers.  

#### Who it suits best
- **EVA fans & collectors** who actually main their gear.  
- **Palm grippers / medium-to-large hands** wanting all-day comfort.  
- **Reliability hunters** who value repairability and switch customization.  

#### Designed alternatives
If the EVA-02 collab vibe is the whole point, but you want to browse other designed wireless mice:

- <pop=mouse-209>Fnatic x Lamzu THORN</pop> — esports-leaning styling in a lighter, more “aim-tuned” ergonomic shell.  
- <pop=mouse-323>Pwnage StormBreaker X BLG Valorant</pop> — a flashier collab build aimed at collectors who still queue.  
- <pop=mouse-127>Razer Viper Mini Signature Edition</pop> — boutique, smaller ambi shape that feels more like a “display piece” flex pick.  

**Bottom line:** If you want Unit-02 desk art you can main in ranked, this is one of the few collab mice that doesn’t force a choice between looks and capability. We scored it **slighly higher** than the standard Gladius III Wireless AimPoint for the excellent design execution and included grip tape—just keep an eye on the scroll wheel over time.
pros: ASUS’s official Evangelion collaboration series (EVA-01 → EVA-02)
EVA-02 aesthetics and included grip tape add real collector and practical value
**Push-Fit Switch Socket II** allows instant, solder-free switch replacement (mechanical or optical)
**AimPoint sensor (650 IPS, 50 G)** delivers class-leading wireless tracking
Excellent battery life (~119 hours 2.4 GHz with RGB off)
cons: Scroll wheel encoder can develop reverse-scroll “kickback” over time and is not hot-swappable
79 g weight, on the heaver side
Armoury Crate software remains resource-heavy; best used for initial setup only
High button profile (44 mm) can feel awkward for small hands or fingertip grips
Polling rate capped at 1000 Hz (no 4 K/8 K support)
